757	    (hP' : ∀ i, P'.map (t i) ≤ P') (p : MvPolynomial (Fin r) κ) :
758	    ∀ m ∈ P', (polyEndHom t hcomm p) m ∈ P' := by
759	  induction p using MvPolynomial.induction_on with
760	  | C a => intro m hm; rw [polyEndHom_C]; simpa using P'.smul_mem a hm
761	  | add p q hp hq =>
762	      intro m hm; rw [map_add, LinearMap.add_apply]; exact P'.add_mem (hp m hm) (hq m hm)
763	  | mul_X p i hp =>
764	      intro m hm; rw [map_mul, polyEndHom_X, Module.End.mul_apply]
765	      exact hp _ (hP' i (Submodule.mem_map_of_mem hm))
766	
767	/-- **Mod-`P'` semilinearity heart.** For `m ∈ P`, evaluating a polynomial `s` via the full
768	endomorphism family `t` agrees, modulo `P'`, with first projecting away the last variable
769	(`lastVarAlgHom`) and evaluating via `t ∘ Fin.castSucc` — provided the last endomorphism
770	`x = t (Fin.last r)` carries `P` into `P'` and `P, P'` are stable under every `t i`. This is the
771	algebraic content of the finiteness transfer (`lem:graded_subquotient_finite_transfer`). -/
772	lemma polyEndHom_lastVar_sub_mem {r : ℕ} (t : Fin (r + 1) → Module.End κ M)
773	    (hcomm : ∀ i j, Commute (t i) (t j)) {P P' : Submodule κ M}
774	    (hP : ∀ i, P.map (t i) ≤ P) (hP' : ∀ i, P'.map (t i) ≤ P')
775	    (hannih : P.map (t (Fin.last r)) ≤ P')
776	    (s : MvPolynomial (Fin (r + 1)) κ) :
777	    ∀ m ∈ P, (polyEndHom t hcomm s) m
778	      - (polyEndHom (fun i => t (Fin.castSucc i)) (fun i j => hcomm _ _)
779	          (lastVarAlgHom r κ s)) m ∈ P' := by
780	  induction s using MvPolynomial.induction_on with
781	  | C a =>
782	      intro m _
783	      rw [lastVarAlgHom_C, polyEndHom_C, polyEndHom_C, sub_self]
784	      exact P'.zero_mem
785	  | add p q hp hq =>
786	      intro m hm
787	      rw [map_add, map_add, map_add, LinearMap.add_apply, LinearMap.add_apply]
788	      have := P'.add_mem (hp m hm) (hq m hm)
789	      convert this using 1
790	      abel
791	  | mul_X p j hp =>
792	      intro m hm
793	      rw [map_mul, polyEndHom_X, Module.End.mul_apply]
794	      rcases Fin.eq_castSucc_or_eq_last j with ⟨i, rfl⟩ | rfl
795	      · -- `j = castSucc i`: reduce to the IH at `t (castSucc i) m ∈ P`
796	        rw [map_mul, lastVarAlgHom_X_castSucc, map_mul, polyEndHom_X, Module.End.mul_apply]
797	        exact hp _ (hP _ (Submodule.mem_map_of_mem hm))
798	      · -- `j = last`: the right term vanishes; the left lands in `P'` by annihilation
799	        rw [map_mul, lastVarAlgHom_X_last, mul_zero, map_zero, LinearMap.zero_apply, sub_zero]
800	        exact polyEndHom_mem_of_stable t hcomm hP' p _
801	          (hannih (Submodule.mem_map_of_mem hm))
802	
803	/-- **Finiteness transfer down one variable (core).** If the subquotient `P/P'` (carriers
804	ambient submodules of `M`, stable under every `t i`) is finite over `MvPolynomial (Fin (r+1)) κ`
805	and the last endomorphism `t (Fin.last r)` carries `P` into `P'`, then `P/P'` is finite over
806	`MvPolynomial (Fin r) κ` for the action of `t ∘ Fin.castSucc`. The action factors through the
807	`lastVarAlgHom` surjection; finiteness transfers by `Module.Finite.of_surjective`. -/
808	lemma subquotient_finite_transfer {r : ℕ} (t : Fin (r + 1) → Module.End κ M)
809	    (hcomm : ∀ i j, Commute (t i) (t j)) {P P' : Submodule κ M}
810	    (hP : ∀ i, P.map (t i) ≤ P) (hP' : ∀ i, P'.map (t i) ≤ P')
811	    (hannih : P.map (t (Fin.last r)) ≤ P')
812	    (hpar : letI := polyModule t hcomm
813	      Module.Finite (MvPolynomial (Fin (r + 1)) κ)
814	        (↥(polySubmodule t hcomm P hP) ⧸
815	          (polySubmodule t hcomm P' hP').comap (polySubmodule t hcomm P hP).subtype)) :
816	    letI := polyModule (fun i => t (Fin.castSucc i)) (fun i j => hcomm _ _)
817	    Module.Finite (MvPolynomial (Fin r) κ)
818	      (↥(polySubmodule (fun i => t (Fin.castSucc i)) (fun i j => hcomm _ _) P
819	            (fun i => hP _)) ⧸
820	        (polySubmodule (fun i => t (Fin.castSucc i)) (fun i j => hcomm _ _) P'
821	            (fun i => hP' _)).comap
822	          (polySubmodule (fun i => t (Fin.castSucc i)) (fun i j => hcomm _ _) P
823	            (fun i => hP _)).subtype) := by
824	  classical
825	  letI iS := polyModule t hcomm
826	  letI iS' := polyModule (fun i => t (Fin.castSucc i)) (fun i j => hcomm _ _)
827	  haveI := hpar
828	  set σ : MvPolynomial (Fin (r + 1)) κ →+* MvPolynomial (Fin r) κ :=
829	    (lastVarAlgHom r κ).toRingHom with hσ
830	  -- the σ-semilinear map out of the numerator `↥Pbig` into the target quotient `Q^S'`
831	  set g : ↥(polySubmodule t hcomm P hP) →ₛₗ[σ]
832	      (↥(polySubmodule (fun i => t (Fin.castSucc i)) (fun i j => hcomm _ _) P (fun i => hP _)) ⧸
833	        (polySubmodule (fun i => t (Fin.castSucc i)) (fun i j => hcomm _ _) P'
834	            (fun i => hP' _)).comap
835	          (polySubmodule (fun i => t (Fin.castSucc i)) (fun i j => hcomm _ _) P
836	            (fun i => hP _)).subtype) :=
837	    { toFun := fun y => Submodule.Quotient.mk ⟨(y : M), y.2⟩
838	      map_add' := fun a b => by rw [← Submodule.Quotient.mk_add]; rfl
839	      map_smul' := by
840	        intro s y
841	        rw [← Submodule.Quotient.mk_smul, Submodule.Quotient.eq, Submodule.mem_comap]
842	        change (polyEndHom t hcomm s) (y : M)
843	          - (polyEndHom (fun i => t (Fin.castSucc i)) (fun i j => hcomm _ _) (σ s)) (y : M) ∈ P'
844	        exact polyEndHom_lastVar_sub_mem t hcomm hP hP' hannih s (y : M) y.2 }
845	    with hg
846	  have hgsurj : Function.Surjective g := by
847	    intro z
848	    refine Submodule.Quotient.induction_on _ z (fun y => ?_)
849	    exact ⟨⟨(y : M), y.2⟩, rfl⟩
850	  refine Module.Finite.of_surjective
851	    (Submodule.liftQ ((polySubmodule t hcomm P' hP').comap (polySubmodule t hcomm P hP).subtype)
852	      g ?_) ?_
853	  · -- the denominator `K` is killed by `g`
854	    intro y hy
855	    rw [LinearMap.mem_ker, hg]
856	    simp only [LinearMap.coe_mk, AddHom.coe_mk]
857	    rw [Submodule.Quotient.mk_eq_zero, Submodule.mem_comap]
858	    exact (Submodule.mem_comap).mp hy
859	  · -- `liftQ` of a surjection is surjective
860	    intro z
861	    obtain ⟨y, hy⟩ := hgsurj z
862	    exact ⟨Submodule.Quotient.mk y, by rw [Submodule.liftQ_apply]; exact hy⟩
863	
864	/-- Enlarging the denominator keeps `S`-finiteness: `N/P₂` is a quotient of `N/P₁` when
865	`P₁ ≤ P₂`, so finiteness of the latter transfers along the surjection. -/
866	lemma polyQuot_finite_of_le_denominator {r : ℕ} (t : Fin r → Module.End κ M)
867	    (hcomm : ∀ i j, Commute (t i) (t j)) {N P₁ P₂ : Submodule κ M}
868	    (hN : ∀ i, N.map (t i) ≤ N) (hP₁ : ∀ i, P₁.map (t i) ≤ P₁) (hP₂ : ∀ i, P₂.map (t i) ≤ P₂)
869	    (h12 : P₁ ≤ P₂)
870	    (hfin : letI := polyModule t hcomm
871	      Module.Finite (MvPolynomial (Fin r) κ)
872	        (↥(polySubmodule t hcomm N hN) ⧸
873	          (polySubmodule t hcomm P₁ hP₁).comap (polySubmodule t hcomm N hN).subtype)) :
874	    letI := polyModule t hcomm
875	    Module.Finite (MvPolynomial (Fin r) κ)
876	      (↥(polySubmodule t hcomm N hN) ⧸
877	        (polySubmodule t hcomm P₂ hP₂).comap (polySubmodule t hcomm N hN).subtype) := by
878	  letI := polyModule t hcomm
879	  haveI := hfin
880	  refine Module.Finite.of_surjective
881	    (Submodule.liftQ ((polySubmodule t hcomm P₁ hP₁).comap (polySubmodule t hcomm N hN).subtype)
882	      ((polySubmodule t hcomm P₂ hP₂).comap (polySubmodule t hcomm N hN).subtype).mkQ ?_) ?_
883	  · rw [Submodule.ker_mkQ]
884	    exact Submodule.comap_mono (fun x hx => h12 hx)
885	  · intro z
886	    obtain ⟨y, rfl⟩ := Submodule.Quotient.mk_surjective _ z
887	    exact ⟨Submodule.Quotient.mk y, rfl⟩
888	
889	/-- Shrinking the numerator keeps `S`-finiteness: `N₁/P'` embeds as an `S`-submodule of `N₂/P'`
890	when `N₁ ≤ N₂`, and a submodule of a Noetherian (finite over a Noetherian ring) module is
891	finite. -/
892	lemma polyQuot_finite_of_le_numerator {r : ℕ} (t : Fin r → Module.End κ M)
893	    (hcomm : ∀ i j, Commute (t i) (t j)) {N₁ N₂ P' : Submodule κ M}
894	    (hN₁ : ∀ i, N₁.map (t i) ≤ N₁) (hN₂ : ∀ i, N₂.map (t i) ≤ N₂) (hP' : ∀ i, P'.map (t i) ≤ P')
895	    (h12 : N₁ ≤ N₂)
896	    (hfin : letI := polyModule t hcomm
897	      Module.Finite (MvPolynomial (Fin r) κ)
898	        (↥(polySubmodule t hcomm N₂ hN₂) ⧸
899	          (polySubmodule t hcomm P' hP').comap (polySubmodule t hcomm N₂ hN₂).subtype)) :
900	    letI := polyModule t hcomm
901	    Module.Finite (MvPolynomial (Fin r) κ)
902	      (↥(polySubmodule t hcomm N₁ hN₁) ⧸
903	        (polySubmodule t hcomm P' hP').comap (polySubmodule t hcomm N₁ hN₁).subtype) := by
904	  letI := polyModule t hcomm
905	  haveI : IsNoetherianRing (MvPolynomial (Fin r) κ) := MvPolynomial.isNoetherianRing_fin
906	  haveI := hfin
907	  haveI : _root_.IsNoetherian (MvPolynomial (Fin r) κ)
908	      (↥(polySubmodule t hcomm N₂ hN₂) ⧸
909	        (polySubmodule t hcomm P' hP').comap (polySubmodule t hcomm N₂ hN₂).subtype) :=
910	    isNoetherian_of_isNoetherianRing_of_finite _ _
911	  -- the inclusion of numerators descends to an injective `S`-linear map of quotients
912	  set incl : ↥(polySubmodule t hcomm N₁ hN₁) →ₗ[MvPolynomial (Fin r) κ]
913	      ↥(polySubmodule t hcomm N₂ hN₂) :=
914	    Submodule.inclusion (fun x hx => h12 hx) with hincl
915	  refine Module.Finite.of_injective
916	    (Submodule.mapQ ((polySubmodule t hcomm P' hP').comap (polySubmodule t hcomm N₁ hN₁).subtype)
917	      ((polySubmodule t hcomm P' hP').comap (polySubmodule t hcomm N₂ hN₂).subtype) incl ?_) ?_
918	  · intro y hy
919	    rw [Submodule.mem_comap] at hy ⊢
920	    exact hy
921	  · rw [← LinearMap.ker_eq_bot, eq_bot_iff]
922	    intro z hz
923	    induction z using Submodule.Quotient.induction_on with
924	    | _ y =>
925	      rw [LinearMap.mem_ker, Submodule.mapQ_apply, Submodule.Quotient.mk_eq_zero,
926	        Submodule.mem_comap] at hz
927	      rw [Submodule.mem_bot, Submodule.Quotient.mk_eq_zero, Submodule.mem_comap]
928	      exact hz
929	
930	end Transfer
931	
932	/-! ### The ambient subquotient datum
933	
934	Bundles a homogeneous pair `N' ≤ N` of a fixed graded `κ`-module `M = ⨁ ℳ n` with `r`
935	pairwise-commuting degree-raising endomorphisms preserving the pair, plus the finiteness of
936	the represented subquotient `N/N'` over the free polynomial ring `MvPolynomial (Fin r) κ` (via
937	`polySubmodule`, so the underlying carriers stay ambient submodules of `M`). This is the
938	length-`r` carrier of the Stacks 00K1 ambient induction (`def:graded_subquotientHilb`). -/
939	
940	section Datum
941	
942	variable {κ M : Type*} [Field κ] [AddCommGroup M] [Module κ M]
943	variable (ℳ : ℕ → Submodule κ M) [DirectSum.Decomposition ℳ]
944	
945	/-- A length-`r` **ambient subquotient datum** over the fixed graded module `M = ⨁ ℳ n`.
946	Project-local: the carrier of the Stacks 00K1 ambient induction (`def:graded_subquotientHilb`).
947	The finiteness field `hfin` records that the represented subquotient `N/N'` is a finite module
948	over the free polynomial ring `MvPolynomial (Fin r) κ` acting through the `t i`; the carriers
949	involved are the ambient `t`-stable submodules `polySubmodule … N`, `polySubmodule … N'`. -/
950	structure SubquotientDatum (r : ℕ) where
951	  /-- The upper homogeneous submodule. -/
952	  N : Submodule κ M
953	  /-- The lower homogeneous submodule. -/
954	  N' : Submodule κ M
955	  /-- `N'` is contained in `N`. -/
956	  hle : N' ≤ N
957	  /-- `N` is homogeneous. -/
958	  hN : N.IsHomogeneous ℳ
959	  /-- `N'` is homogeneous. -/
960	  hN' : N'.IsHomogeneous ℳ
961	  /-- The `r` degree-raising endomorphisms. -/
962	  t : Fin r → Module.End κ M
963	  /-- They pairwise commute. -/
964	  hcomm : ∀ i j, Commute (t i) (t j)
965	  /-- Each raises degree by one. -/
966	  hraise : ∀ i, RaisesDegree ℳ (t i)
967	  /-- Each preserves `N`. -/
968	  hpresN : ∀ i, N.map (t i) ≤ N
969	  /-- Each preserves `N'`. -/
970	  hpresN' : ∀ i, N'.map (t i) ≤ N'
971	  /-- The represented subquotient `N/N'` is finite over `MvPolynomial (Fin r) κ`. -/
972	  hfin : letI := polyModule t hcomm
973	    Module.Finite (MvPolynomial (Fin r) κ)
974	      (↥(polySubmodule t hcomm N hpresN) ⧸
975	        (polySubmodule t hcomm N' hpresN').comap (polySubmodule t hcomm N hpresN).subtype)
976	
977	/-- The ambient Hilbert function `n ↦ dim_κ(N ⊓ ℳ n) − dim_κ(N' ⊓ ℳ n)` of a subquotient
978	datum (`def:graded_subquotientHilb`). -/
979	noncomputable def SubquotientDatum.hilb {r : ℕ} (D : SubquotientDatum ℳ r) : ℕ → ℚ :=
980	  subquotientHilb ℳ D.N D.N'
981	
982	/-- The kernel pair's lower module `N ⊓ x⁻¹N'` is stable under every endomorphism of the family
983	(needed for the finiteness transfer over the full polynomial ring). -/
984	lemma ker_stable_full {r : ℕ} (D : SubquotientDatum ℳ (r + 1)) (i : Fin (r + 1)) :
985	    (D.N ⊓ (D.N').comap (D.t (Fin.last r))).map (D.t i)
986	      ≤ D.N ⊓ (D.N').comap (D.t (Fin.last r)) :=
987	  le_trans (le_inf (Submodule.map_mono inf_le_left) (Submodule.map_mono inf_le_right))
988	    (inf_le_inf (D.hpresN i) (comap_map_le_of_commute (D.hcomm (Fin.last r) i) (D.hpresN' i)))
989	
990	/-- The cokernel pair's upper module `N' ⊔ x·N` is stable under every endomorphism of the family. -/
991	lemma coker_stable_full {r : ℕ} (D : SubquotientDatum ℳ (r + 1)) (i : Fin (r + 1)) :
992	    (D.N' ⊔ D.N.map (D.t (Fin.last r))).map (D.t i)
993	      ≤ D.N' ⊔ D.N.map (D.t (Fin.last r)) := by
994	  rw [Submodule.map_sup]
995	  exact sup_le_sup (D.hpresN' i)
996	    (map_map_le_of_commute (D.hcomm (Fin.last r) i) (D.hpresN i))
997	
998	/-- **Kernel constructor.** From a length-`(r+1)` subquotient datum, the kernel subquotient
999	`(N ⊓ x⁻¹N', N')` of multiplication by `x = t (last)`, as a length-`r` datum on `t ∘ castSucc`.
1000	All non-finiteness fields are the ambient kernel/cokernel calculus; the finiteness field is the
1001	keystone transfer `subquotient_finite_transfer` (`lem:graded_subquotient_finite_transfer`). -/
1002	noncomputable def SubquotientDatum.ker {r : ℕ} (D : SubquotientDatum ℳ (r + 1)) :
1003	    SubquotientDatum ℳ r where
1004	  N := D.N ⊓ (D.N').comap (D.t (Fin.last r))
1005	  N' := D.N'
1006	  hle := ker_le D.hle (D.hpresN' (Fin.last r))
1007	  hN := ker_isHomogeneous ℳ (D.hraise (Fin.last r)) D.hN D.hN'
1008	  hN' := D.hN'
1009	  t := fun i => D.t (Fin.castSucc i)
1010	  hcomm := fun i j => D.hcomm _ _
1011	  hraise := fun i => D.hraise _
1012	  hpresN := fun i => ker_stable_full ℳ D (Fin.castSucc i)
1013	  hpresN' := fun i => D.hpresN' _
1014	  hfin :=
1015	    subquotient_finite_transfer D.t D.hcomm (ker_stable_full ℳ D) D.hpresN'
1016	      ker_annihilate
1017	      (polyQuot_finite_of_le_numerator D.t D.hcomm (ker_stable_full ℳ D) D.hpresN D.hpresN'
1018	        inf_le_left D.hfin)
1019	
1020	/-- **Cokernel constructor.** From a length-`(r+1)` subquotient datum, the cokernel subquotient
1021	`(N, N' ⊔ x·N)` of multiplication by `x = t (last)`, as a length-`r` datum on `t ∘ castSucc`. -/
1022	noncomputable def SubquotientDatum.coker {r : ℕ} (D : SubquotientDatum ℳ (r + 1)) :
1023	    SubquotientDatum ℳ r where
1024	  N := D.N
1025	  N' := D.N' ⊔ D.N.map (D.t (Fin.last r))
1026	  hle := coker_le D.hle (D.hpresN (Fin.last r))
1027	  hN := D.hN
1028	  hN' := coker_isHomogeneous ℳ (D.hraise (Fin.last r)) D.hN D.hN'
1029	  t := fun i => D.t (Fin.castSucc i)
1030	  hcomm := fun i j => D.hcomm _ _
1031	  hraise := fun i => D.hraise _
1032	  hpresN := fun i => D.hpresN _
1033	  hpresN' := fun i => coker_stable_full ℳ D (Fin.castSucc i)
1034	  hfin :=
1035	    subquotient_finite_transfer D.t D.hcomm D.hpresN (coker_stable_full ℳ D)
1036	      coker_annihilate
1037	      (polyQuot_finite_of_le_denominator D.t D.hcomm D.hpresN D.hpresN' (coker_stable_full ℳ D)
1038	        le_sup_left D.hfin)
1039	
1040	/-- Base-case finiteness: a module finite over `MvPolynomial σ κ` for an *empty* index `σ` —
1041	in particular `σ = Fin 0`, the length-zero subquotient datum — is finite-dimensional over `κ`,
1042	since `MvPolynomial σ κ ≃ₐ[κ] κ`. Project-local: the base case of the Stacks 00K1 induction.
1043	Stated outside the `Datum` section as it needs no grading. -/
1044	lemma finiteDimensional_of_mvPolynomial_isEmpty_finite
1045	    {κ : Type*} [Field κ] {σ : Type*} [IsEmpty σ]
1046	    {Q : Type*} [AddCommGroup Q] [Module κ Q]
1047	    [Module (MvPolynomial σ κ) Q] [IsScalarTower κ (MvPolynomial σ κ) Q]
1048	    [Module.Finite (MvPolynomial σ κ) Q] : FiniteDimensional κ Q := by
1049	  haveI : Module.Finite κ (MvPolynomial σ κ) :=
1050	    Module.Finite.equiv (MvPolynomial.isEmptyAlgEquiv κ σ).symm.toLinearEquiv
1051	  exact Module.Finite.trans (MvPolynomial σ κ) Q
1052	
1053	section Induction
1054	
1055	variable [∀ n, FiniteDimensional κ ↥(ℳ n)]
1056	
1057	/-- **Independence of images modulo the kernel.** If a family `g i` of submodules of `A` is
1058	"independent modulo `ker π`" — every `a ∈ g i` that also lies in `ker π ⊔ ⨆ j ≠ i, g j` already
1059	lies in `ker π` — then the images `π '' (g i)` form an `iSupIndep` family. The image family
1060	inherits the degree-`i` separation of the `g i` once the kernel is quotiented out. Project-local:
1061	the abstract lattice core of the base-case independence
1062	(`lem:graded_subquotient_base_eventuallyZero`, Step 1), kept ring-agnostic so it can be applied to
1063	the `κ`-linear quotient map `↥N ↠ N/N'`. -/
1064	lemma iSupIndep_map_of_mem_ker_sup {F A Q : Type*} [Field F] [AddCommGroup A]
1065	    [Module F A] [AddCommGroup Q] [Module F Q] (π : A →ₗ[F] Q) {ι : Type*} (g : ι → Submodule F A)
1066	    (h : ∀ i, ∀ a ∈ g i, a ∈ LinearMap.ker π ⊔ ⨆ j, ⨆ (_ : j ≠ i), g j → a ∈ LinearMap.ker π) :
1067	    iSupIndep (fun i => Submodule.map π (g i)) := by
1068	  rw [iSupIndep_def]
1069	  intro i
1070	  rw [Submodule.disjoint_def]
1071	  intro q hq1 hq2
1072	  obtain ⟨a, ha, rfl⟩ := hq1
1073	  rw [show (⨆ j, ⨆ (_ : j ≠ i), Submodule.map π (g j))
1074	      = Submodule.map π (⨆ j, ⨆ (_ : j ≠ i), g j) by simp_rw [Submodule.map_iSup]] at hq2
1075	  obtain ⟨b, hb, hbq⟩ := hq2
1076	  have ha' : a ∈ LinearMap.ker π ⊔ ⨆ j, ⨆ (_ : j ≠ i), g j := by
1077	    have hab : a = (a - b) + b := by abel
1078	    rw [hab]
1079	    refine Submodule.add_mem_sup ?_ hb
1080	    rw [LinearMap.mem_ker, map_sub, hbq, sub_self]
1081	  exact (LinearMap.mem_ker).mp (h i a ha ha')
1082	
1083	omit [∀ n, FiniteDimensional κ ↥(ℳ n)] in
1084	/-- **Base case of the ambient subquotient induction.** A length-`0` subquotient datum has an
1085	eventually-zero ambient Hilbert function: the subquotient `N/N'` is finite over
1086	`MvPolynomial (Fin 0) κ ≃ κ`, hence finite-dimensional over `κ`, so its degreewise pieces — an
1087	independent family inside the finite-dimensional quotient — vanish for large degree. -/
1088	lemma subquotient_base_eventuallyZero (D : SubquotientDatum ℳ 0) :
1089	    ∃ K, ∀ n, K < n → subquotientHilb ℳ D.N D.N' n = 0 := by
1090	  classical
1091	  letI := polyModule D.t D.hcomm
1092	  haveI hts : IsScalarTower κ (MvPolynomial (Fin 0) κ) M := polyModule_isScalarTower D.t D.hcomm
1093	  haveI := D.hfin
1094	  -- the subquotient `Q = N/N'` is finite-dimensional over `κ`
1095	  haveI hfd : FiniteDimensional κ (↥(polySubmodule D.t D.hcomm D.N D.hpresN) ⧸
1096	      (polySubmodule D.t D.hcomm D.N' D.hpresN').comap
1097	        (polySubmodule D.t D.hcomm D.N D.hpresN).subtype) :=
1098	    finiteDimensional_of_mvPolynomial_isEmpty_finite (σ := Fin 0)
1099	  set W : Submodule (MvPolynomial (Fin 0) κ) ↥(polySubmodule D.t D.hcomm D.N D.hpresN) :=
1100	    (polySubmodule D.t D.hcomm D.N' D.hpresN').comap
1101	      (polySubmodule D.t D.hcomm D.N D.hpresN).subtype with hW
1102	  set Q := ↥(polySubmodule D.t D.hcomm D.N D.hpresN) ⧸ W with hQ
1103	  -- the degreewise image map `ψ n : N ⊓ ℳ n →ₗ[κ] Q`, `m ↦ [m]`
1104	  set ψ : ∀ n, ↥(D.N ⊓ ℳ n) →ₗ[κ] Q := fun n =>
1105	    { toFun := fun m => Submodule.Quotient.mk ⟨(m : M), (Submodule.mem_inf.mp m.2).1⟩
1106	      map_add' := fun a b => by rw [← Submodule.Quotient.mk_add]; rfl
1107	      map_smul' := fun c a => by
1108	        simp only [RingHom.id_apply, SetLike.val_smul]
1109	        rw [← Submodule.Quotient.mk_smul]
1110	        rfl } with hψ
1111	  -- the ranges form an independent family in the finite-dimensional `Q`
1112	  have hindep : iSupIndep (fun n => LinearMap.range (ψ n)) := by
1113	    -- ROUTE (b): direct membership analysis, reading off the degree-`i` homogeneous component in
1114	    -- the ambient `M` (never building an outgoing `κ`-linear map out of `Q`).
1115	    -- *Core ambient fact.* A homogeneous element of degree `i` lying in `N' ⊔ ⨆_{j≠i} ℳ j` is in
1116	    -- `N'`: its degree-`i` component is itself, equals the degree-`i` component of its `N'`-part
1117	    -- (the `⨆_{j≠i} ℳ j`-part contributes `0` in degree `i`), and `N'` is homogeneous.
1118	    have core : ∀ (i : ℕ) (x : M), x ∈ ℳ i →
1119	        x ∈ D.N' ⊔ (⨆ j, ⨆ (_ : j ≠ i), ℳ j) → x ∈ D.N' := by
1120	      intro i x hxi hxmem
1121	      obtain ⟨u, hu, v, hv, huv⟩ := Submodule.mem_sup.mp hxmem
1122	      set proj : M →ₗ[κ] M :=
1123	        (ℳ i).subtype ∘ₗ (DirectSum.component κ ℕ (fun n => ℳ n) i) ∘ₗ
1124	          (DirectSum.decomposeLinearEquiv ℳ).toLinearMap with hproj
1125	      have hpe : ∀ m : M, proj m = ↑((DirectSum.decompose ℳ m) i) := fun m => rfl
1126	      have hx_eq : proj x = x := by rw [hpe]; exact DirectSum.decompose_of_mem_same ℳ hxi
1127	      have hu_pn : proj u ∈ D.N' := by
1128	        rw [hpe]; exact (Submodule.IsHomogeneous.mem_iff ℳ D.hN').mp hu i
1129	      have hv_zero : proj v = 0 := by
1130	        have hker : (⨆ j, ⨆ (_ : j ≠ i), ℳ j) ≤ LinearMap.ker proj := by
1131	          apply iSup_le; intro j; apply iSup_le; intro hji w hw
1132	          rw [LinearMap.mem_ker, hpe]
1133	          exact DirectSum.decompose_of_mem_ne ℳ hw hji
1134	        exact (LinearMap.mem_ker).mp (hker hv)
1135	      have hsum : proj x = proj u + proj v := by rw [← map_add, huv]
1136	      rw [hx_eq, hv_zero, add_zero] at hsum
1137	      rw [hsum]; exact hu_pn
1138	    -- The `κ`-linear quotient map `Φ : ↥N ↠ Q = N/N'` (the un-restricted form of `ψ`).
1139	    set Φ : ↥(D.N) →ₗ[κ] Q :=
1140	      { toFun := fun c => Submodule.Quotient.mk ⟨(c : M), c.2⟩
1141	        map_add' := fun a b => by rw [← Submodule.Quotient.mk_add]; rfl
1142	        map_smul' := fun c a => by
1143	          simp only [RingHom.id_apply, SetLike.val_smul]
1144	          rw [← Submodule.Quotient.mk_smul]; rfl } with hΦ
1145	    -- The lifted source pieces `g n ⊆ ↥N` corresponding to `N ⊓ ℳ n`.
1146	    set g : ℕ → Submodule κ ↥(D.N) :=
1147	      fun n => Submodule.comap D.N.subtype (D.N ⊓ ℳ n) with hg
1148	    -- `range (ψ n) = map Φ (g n)`: `ψ n` is `Φ` precomposed with the inclusion of `N ⊓ ℳ n`.
1149	    have hrange : ∀ n, (ψ n).range = Submodule.map Φ (g n) := by
1150	      intro n
1151	      have hcomp : ψ n = Φ ∘ₗ Submodule.inclusion (inf_le_left : D.N ⊓ ℳ n ≤ D.N) := by
1152	        ext m; rfl
1153	      rw [hcomp, LinearMap.range_comp, Submodule.range_inclusion]
1154	    rw [show (fun n => (ψ n).range) = (fun n => Submodule.map Φ (g n)) from funext hrange]
1155	    -- Apply the abstract independence-modulo-kernel lemma; discharge its hypothesis via `core`.
1156	    apply iSupIndep_map_of_mem_ker_sup Φ g
1157	    intro i a ha hmem
1158	    have hai : (a : M) ∈ ℳ i := (Submodule.mem_inf.mp ((Submodule.mem_comap).mp ha)).2
1159	    -- Push the `↥N`-level membership down to `M` along the subtype.
1160	    have hpush : (a : M) ∈ D.N' ⊔ (⨆ j, ⨆ (_ : j ≠ i), ℳ j) := by
1161	      have hle : Submodule.map D.N.subtype (LinearMap.ker Φ ⊔ ⨆ j, ⨆ (_ : j ≠ i), g j)
1162	          ≤ D.N' ⊔ (⨆ j, ⨆ (_ : j ≠ i), ℳ j) := by
1163	        rw [Submodule.map_sup]
1164	        refine sup_le_sup ?_ ?_
1165	        · rintro y ⟨c, hc, rfl⟩
1166	          rw [SetLike.mem_coe, LinearMap.mem_ker, hΦ] at hc
1167	          simp only [LinearMap.coe_mk, AddHom.coe_mk] at hc
1168	          rw [Submodule.Quotient.mk_eq_zero, hW, Submodule.mem_comap] at hc
1169	          exact hc
1170	        · rw [Submodule.map_iSup]
1171	          refine iSup_mono fun j => ?_
1172	          rw [Submodule.map_iSup]
1173	          refine iSup_mono fun hji => ?_
1174	          calc Submodule.map D.N.subtype (g j) ≤ D.N ⊓ ℳ j := Submodule.map_comap_le _ _
1175	            _ ≤ ℳ j := inf_le_right
1176	      exact hle ⟨a, hmem, rfl⟩
1177	    have hmemN' : (a : M) ∈ D.N' := core i a hai hpush
1178	    rw [LinearMap.mem_ker, hΦ]
1179	    simp only [LinearMap.coe_mk, AddHom.coe_mk]
1180	    rw [Submodule.Quotient.mk_eq_zero, hW, Submodule.mem_comap]
1181	    exact hmemN'
1182	  haveI : _root_.IsNoetherian κ Q := isNoetherian_of_isNoetherianRing_of_finite κ Q
1183	  have hfinset := Submodule.finite_ne_bot_of_iSupIndep hindep
1184	  obtain ⟨K, hK⟩ := hfinset.bddAbove
1185	  refine ⟨K, fun n hn => ?_⟩
1186	  -- beyond `K`, `range (ψ n) = ⊥`, forcing `N ⊓ ℳ n ≤ N'`, hence the Hilbert value is `0`
1187	  have hbot : LinearMap.range (ψ n) = ⊥ := by
1188	    by_contra h
1189	    exact absurd (hK h) (not_le.mpr hn)
1190	  have hsub : D.N ⊓ ℳ n ≤ D.N' := by
1191	    intro m hm
1192	    have h0 : ψ n ⟨m, hm⟩ = 0 := by
1193	      have hmem : ψ n ⟨m, hm⟩ ∈ LinearMap.range (ψ n) := LinearMap.mem_range_self _ _
1194	      rw [hbot, Submodule.mem_bot] at hmem; exact hmem
1195	    have h1 : (⟨m, (Submodule.mem_inf.mp hm).1⟩ :
1196	        ↥(polySubmodule D.t D.hcomm D.N D.hpresN)) ∈ W := by
1197	      rw [← Submodule.Quotient.mk_eq_zero]; exact h0
1198	    rw [hW, Submodule.mem_comap] at h1
1199	    exact h1
1200	  have heq : D.N ⊓ ℳ n = D.N' ⊓ ℳ n :=
1201	    le_antisymm (le_inf hsub inf_le_right) (inf_le_inf D.hle le_rfl)
1202	  have hfr : Module.finrank κ ↥(D.N ⊓ ℳ n) = Module.finrank κ ↥(D.N' ⊓ ℳ n) := by rw [heq]
1203	  simp only [subquotientHilb, hfr, sub_self, Int.cast_zero]
1204	
1205	/-- **The ambient subquotient induction (Stacks 00K1).** The ambient Hilbert function of a
1206	length-`r` subquotient datum is a rational Hilbert function of order `r`
1207	(`lem:graded_subquotient_isRatHilb`). Induction on `r`: the base case is the eventually-zero
1208	function; the step feeds the kernel/cokernel data (`SubquotientDatum.ker`, `.coker`) and the
1209	degreewise difference identity into `IsRatHilb.ofDiffEq`. -/
1210	lemma subquotient_hilbertSeries_rational :
1211	    ∀ {r : ℕ} (D : SubquotientDatum ℳ r), IsRatHilb (SubquotientDatum.hilb ℳ D) r := by
1212	  intro r
1213	  induction r with
1214	  | zero =>
1215	      intro D
1216	      obtain ⟨K, hK⟩ := subquotient_base_eventuallyZero ℳ D
1217	      exact IsRatHilb.ofEventuallyZero K hK
1218	  | succ r ih =>
1219	      intro D
1220	      have hx : RaisesDegree ℳ (D.t (Fin.last r)) := D.hraise _
1221	      refine IsRatHilb.ofDiffEq (N := 0) (ih (SubquotientDatum.coker ℳ D))
1222	        (ih (SubquotientDatum.ker ℳ D)) ?_
1223	      intro n _
1224	      change subquotientHilb ℳ D.N D.N' (n + 1) - subquotientHilb ℳ D.N D.N' n
1225	        = subquotientHilb ℳ D.N (D.N' ⊔ D.N.map (D.t (Fin.last r))) (n + 1)
1226	          - subquotientHilb ℳ (D.N ⊓ (D.N').comap (D.t (Fin.last r))) D.N' n
1227	      exact subquotient_degreewise_diff ℳ hx D.hN D.hN' n
1228	
1229	end Induction
1230	
1231	end Datum
1232	
1233	end GradedModule
1234	
1235	/-- **Graded Hilbert–Serre: rationality of the Hilbert series** (`lem:gradedHilbertSerre_rational`).
1236	For a graded `κ`-module `M = ⨁ ℳ n` with finite-dimensional components, equipped with `r`
1237	pairwise-commuting degree-one endomorphisms (the degree-one generators of the action) for which `M`
1238	is finite over the free polynomial ring `MvPolynomial (Fin r) κ`, the Hilbert function
1239	`n ↦ dim_κ ℳ n` is a rational Hilbert function of order `r`: there are `p ∈ ℚ[X]` and `N` with
1240	`dim_κ ℳ n = [Xⁿ](p · (1 - X)⁻ʳ)` for all `n > N`. This is the substantive (non-Mathlib) half of
1241	graded Hilbert–Serre; it is obtained from the ambient subquotient induction
1242	(`GradedModule.subquotient_hilbertSeries_rational`) applied to the top datum `(⊤, ⊥)`. -/
1243	lemma gradedModule_hilbertSeries_rational {κ M : Type*} [Field κ] [AddCommGroup M] [Module κ M]
1244	    (ℳ : ℕ → Submodule κ M) [DirectSum.Decomposition ℳ] [∀ n, FiniteDimensional κ ↥(ℳ n)]
1245	    {r : ℕ} (t : Fin r → Module.End κ M) (hcomm : ∀ i j, Commute (t i) (t j))
1246	    (hraise : ∀ i, GradedModule.RaisesDegree ℳ (t i))
1247	    (hfin : letI := GradedModule.polyModule t hcomm
1248	      Module.Finite (MvPolynomial (Fin r) κ) M) :
1249	    IsRatHilb (fun n => (Module.finrank κ ↥(ℳ n) : ℚ)) r := by
1250	  letI := GradedModule.polyModule t hcomm
1251	  haveI := hfin
1252	  -- the top datum `(⊤, ⊥)`: its finiteness is exactly `M` finite over the polynomial ring
1253	  have hfintop : Module.Finite (MvPolynomial (Fin r) κ)
1254	      (↥(GradedModule.polySubmodule t hcomm ⊤ (fun _ => le_top)) ⧸
1255	        (GradedModule.polySubmodule t hcomm ⊥ (fun _ => by rw [Submodule.map_bot])).comap
1256	          (GradedModule.polySubmodule t hcomm ⊤ (fun _ => le_top)).subtype) := by
1257	    refine Module.Finite.of_surjective
1258	      ({ toFun := fun m => Submodule.Quotient.mk ⟨m, Submodule.mem_top⟩
1259	         map_add' := fun a b => by rw [← Submodule.Quotient.mk_add]; rfl
1260	         map_smul' := fun c a => by rw [← Submodule.Quotient.mk_smul]; rfl } :
1261	        M →ₗ[MvPolynomial (Fin r) κ] _) ?_
1262	    intro z
1263	    refine Submodule.Quotient.induction_on _ z (fun y => ⟨(y : M), rfl⟩)
1264	  set D : GradedModule.SubquotientDatum ℳ r :=
1265	    { N := ⊤
1266	      N' := ⊥
1267	      hle := bot_le
1268	      hN := by intro i x _; exact Submodule.mem_top
1269	      hN' := by intro i x hx; rw [Submodule.mem_bot] at hx; subst hx; simp
1270	      t := t
1271	      hcomm := hcomm
1272	      hraise := hraise
1273	      hpresN := fun _ => le_top
1274	      hpresN' := fun _ => by rw [Submodule.map_bot]
1275	      hfin := hfintop } with hD
1276	  have hrat := GradedModule.subquotient_hilbertSeries_rational ℳ D
1277	  have heq : GradedModule.SubquotientDatum.hilb ℳ D
1278	      = fun n => (Module.finrank κ ↥(ℳ n) : ℚ) := by
1279	    funext n
1280	    change (((Module.finrank κ ↥((⊤ : Submodule κ M) ⊓ ℳ n) : ℤ)
1281	        - (Module.finrank κ ↥((⊥ : Submodule κ M) ⊓ ℳ n) : ℤ) : ℤ) : ℚ)
1282	      = (Module.finrank κ ↥(ℳ n) : ℚ)
1283	    rw [top_inf_eq, bot_inf_eq, finrank_bot]
1284	    simp
1285	  rwa [heq] at hrat
1286

100	private noncomputable abbrev unitModule (X : Scheme.{u}) : X.Modules :=
101	  SheafOfModules.unit X.ringCatSheaf
102	
103	/-- The `m`-th tensor power `L^{⊗m}` of a sheaf of modules over a scheme, defined
104	by recursion: `L^{⊗0} = 𝒪_X` (the unit module) and
105	`L^{⊗(m+1)} = L^{⊗m} ⊗ L`.  See [Stacks, Tag 01CU] (`def:sheafTensorPow`). -/
106	noncomputable def tensorPow (L : X.Modules) : ℕ → X.Modules
107	  | 0 => unitModule X
108	  | (m + 1) => tensorObj (tensorPow L m) L
109	
110	@[simp] private lemma tensorPow_zero (L : X.Modules) : tensorPow L 0 = unitModule X := rfl
111	
112	@[simp] private lemma tensorPow_succ (L : X.Modules) (m : ℕ) :
113	    tensorPow L (m + 1) = tensorObj (tensorPow L m) L := rfl
114	
115	/-- The `m`-twist `F(m) = F ⊗ L^{⊗m}` of a sheaf of modules `F` by the `m`-th
116	tensor power of a line bundle `L` (`def:sheafModuleTwist`).  This is the
117	degree-`m` carrier of the section graded module. -/
118	noncomputable def moduleTensorPow (F L : X.Modules) (m : ℕ) : X.Modules :=
119	  tensorObj F (tensorPow L m)
120	
121	@[simp] private lemma moduleTensorPow_zero (F L : X.Modules) :
122	    moduleTensorPow F L 0 = tensorObj F (unitModule X) := rfl
123	
124	/-! ### Unitor and braiding isomorphisms of the sheaf tensor product
125	
126	These are the parts of the (would-be) symmetric monoidal structure on `X.Modules`
127	that descend through sheafification from `PresheafOfModules.monoidalCategory`
128	using only *functoriality* of `sheafification` (and, for the unitors, the
129	reflective counit iso) — no strong-monoidality of `sheafification` is needed, so
130	they are axiom-clean.  They are the launching pad for `tensorPowAdd`. -/
131	
132	/-- The counit isomorphism of the module sheafification adjunction: sheafifying
133	the underlying presheaf of a sheaf of modules returns the sheaf itself.  This is
134	an isomorphism because the counit of `sheafification ⊣ toPresheafOfModules` is
135	invertible (the right adjoint `SheafOfModules.forget` is fully faithful).  It is
136	the launching pad for the left-unitor base case of `tensorPowAdd`. -/
137	private noncomputable def sheafificationCounitIso (G : X.Modules) :
138	    sheafification.obj ((toPresheafOfModules X).obj G) ≅ G :=
139	  (asIso (PresheafOfModules.sheafificationAdjunction
140	    (𝟙 X.ringCatSheaf.obj)).counit).app G
141	
142	/-- The left-unitor isomorphism `unitModule X ⊗ G ≅ G` of the sheaf tensor
143	product: the presheaf left unitor `λ_` descended through sheafification, composed
144	with the counit iso `sheafificationCounitIso`.  This is the base case (`m = 0`) of
145	`tensorPowAdd`.  Axiom-clean. -/
146	private noncomputable def tensorObjUnitIso (G : X.Modules) :
147	    tensorObj (unitModule X) G ≅ G :=
148	  sheafification.mapIso
149	      (MonoidalCategory.leftUnitor (C := MonoidalPresheaf X)
150	        ((toPresheafOfModules X).obj G)) ≪≫
151	    sheafificationCounitIso G
152	
153	/-- The right-unitor isomorphism `G ⊗ unitModule X ≅ G` of the sheaf tensor
154	product: the presheaf right unitor `ρ_` descended through sheafification, composed
155	with the counit iso `sheafificationCounitIso`.  Axiom-clean (no monoidal structure
156	on `X.Modules` is required). -/
157	private noncomputable def tensorObjRightUnitor (G : X.Modules) :
158	    tensorObj G (unitModule X) ≅ G :=
159	  sheafification.mapIso
160	      (MonoidalCategory.rightUnitor (C := MonoidalPresheaf X)
161	        ((toPresheafOfModules X).obj G)) ≪≫
162	    sheafificationCounitIso G
163	
164	/-- The braiding isomorphism `F ⊗ G ≅ G ⊗ F` of the sheaf tensor product,
165	descended through sheafification from the symmetric braiding on
166	`X.PresheafOfModules` (`PresheafOfModules.monoidalCategory`).  Axiom-clean: the
167	braiding is pure sheafification-functoriality of the presheaf-level braiding, so
168	no monoidal structure on `X.Modules` is required.  This is the symmetry used in
169	the inductive step of `tensorPowAdd`. -/
170	private noncomputable def tensorBraiding (F G : X.Modules) :
171	    tensorObj F G ≅ tensorObj G F :=
172	  sheafification.mapIso
173	    (BraidedCategory.braiding (C := MonoidalPresheaf X)
174	      ((toPresheafOfModules X).obj F) ((toPresheafOfModules X).obj G))
175	
176	/-! ### Lax-monoidal global sections: the section multiplication
177	
178	The global-sections functor `Γ(X, -)` is only *lax* monoidal: a pair of global
179	sections does not commute with sheafification, so the multiplication is a map,
180	not an isomorphism.  It is nonetheless `Γ(X, 𝒪_X)`-linear and is the data the
181	section graded ring is built from. -/
182	
183	/-- The **section multiplication** (`def:sectionMul`), the `Γ(X,𝒪_X)`-bilinear map
184	`Γ(X,F) ⊗_{Γ(X,𝒪_X)} Γ(X,G) → Γ(X, F ⊗ G)`.
185	
186	Its domain `(F.toPresheaf ⊗ G.toPresheaf)(X)` is, by the objectwise formula of
187	`PresheafOfModules.monoidalCategory`, the `Γ(X,𝒪_X)`-module
188	`Γ(X,F) ⊗_{Γ(X,𝒪_X)} Γ(X,G)` of elementary tensors of global sections; a pair
189	`(σ, τ)` is sent to `σ ⊗ τ`.  Postcomposing with the global-sections component of
190	the sheafification unit `η : P → P^#` (`def:sheafTensorObj`) lands in
191	`Γ(X, F ⊗ G)`.  As a morphism in `ModuleCat (Γ(X,𝒪_X))` it is automatically
192	`Γ(X,𝒪_X)`-bilinear; this records that linearity.  Axiom-clean: it is pure
193	sheafification-unit naturality, requiring no monoidal structure on `X.Modules`. -/
194	noncomputable def sectionsMul (F G : X.Modules) :
195	    (MonoidalCategory.tensorObj (C := MonoidalPresheaf X)
196	        ((toPresheafOfModules X).obj F) ((toPresheafOfModules X).obj G)).obj (Opposite.op ⊤) ⟶
197	      (tensorObj F G).val.obj (Opposite.op ⊤) :=
198	  ((PresheafOfModules.sheafificationAdjunction (𝟙 X.ringCatSheaf.obj)).unit.app
199	      (MonoidalCategory.tensorObj (C := MonoidalPresheaf X)
200	        ((toPresheafOfModules X).obj F) ((toPresheafOfModules X).obj G))).app (Opposite.op ⊤)
201	
202	/-! ### The strong-monoidality comparison `isIso_sheafification_whiskerRight_unit`
203	
204	Following `analogies/snap-route.md` (Analogue 1) and the blueprint proof of
205	`lem:isIso_sheafification_whiskerRight_unit`: module sheafification is the
206	localization functor at the class `W := J.W.inverseImage (toPresheaf R₀)` of
207	morphisms of presheaves of modules whose underlying abelian-presheaf morphism is a
208	local isomorphism (a `J.W` for the opens topology `J` on `X`). -/
209	
210	/-- The Grothendieck topology on the opens of the scheme `X`. -/
211	private abbrev opensTopology (X : Scheme.{u}) : GrothendieckTopology (TopologicalSpace.Opens X) :=
212	  Opens.grothendieckTopology (X : TopCat)
213	
214	open MorphismProperty in
215	/-- **Localization criterion for module sheafification.**  The scheme-level module
216	sheafification `sheafification.map f` is an isomorphism of sheaves of modules iff the
217	underlying abelian-presheaf morphism `(PresheafOfModules.toPresheaf _).map f` lies in
218	the weak-equivalence class `J.W` of the opens topology (i.e. is a local isomorphism of
219	abelian-group presheaves).  This is the reduction step of
220	`isIso_sheafification_whiskerRight_unit`: it turns the strong-monoidality comparison
221	into a purely abelian local-isomorphism statement.  Project-local: it specialises
222	`_root_.PresheafOfModules.inverseImage_W_toPresheaf_eq_inverseImage_isomorphisms` to the
223	identity morphism of `X.ringCatSheaf.obj`. -/
224	lemma isIso_sheafification_map_iff {P Q : X.PresheafOfModules} (f : P ⟶ Q) :
225	    IsIso (sheafification.map f) ↔
226	      (opensTopology X).W ((PresheafOfModules.toPresheaf X.ringCatSheaf.obj).map f) := by
227	  have e := _root_.PresheafOfModules.inverseImage_W_toPresheaf_eq_inverseImage_isomorphisms
228	      (J := opensTopology X) (𝟙 X.ringCatSheaf.obj)
229	  constructor
230	  · intro h
231	    have h' : ((MorphismProperty.isomorphisms (SheafOfModules X.ringCatSheaf)).inverseImage
232	        (PresheafOfModules.sheafification (𝟙 X.ringCatSheaf.obj))) f := h
233	    rw [← e] at h'
234	    exact h'
235	  · intro h
236	    have h' : (((opensTopology X).W).inverseImage
237	        (PresheafOfModules.toPresheaf X.ringCatSheaf.obj)) f := h
238	    rw [e] at h'
239	    exact h'
240	
241	/-- **The sheafification unit is an abelian local isomorphism.**  The underlying
242	abelian-presheaf morphism of the module sheafification unit `η_P : P ⟶ P^#` is
243	*definitionally* the abelian sheafification unit `toSheafify J P.presheaf`
244	(`PresheafOfModules.toPresheaf_map_sheafificationAdjunction_unit_app`), which lies
245	in the weak-equivalence class `J.W` of the opens topology by
246	`GrothendieckTopology.W_toSheafify`.  Project-local: this is the `η_P ∈ J.W`
247	ingredient of the abelian-`J.W`-monoidality transfer underlying
248	`isIso_sheafification_whiskerRight_unit`. -/
249	lemma localIso_toPresheaf_map_unit (P : X.PresheafOfModules) :
250	    (opensTopology X).W ((PresheafOfModules.toPresheaf X.ringCatSheaf.obj).map
251	      ((PresheafOfModules.sheafificationAdjunction (𝟙 X.ringCatSheaf.obj)).unit.app P)) := by
252	  rw [PresheafOfModules.toPresheaf_map_sheafificationAdjunction_unit_app]
253	  exact (opensTopology X).W_toSheafify _
254	
255	/-- **Sheafification inverts the localization unit.**  `sheafification.map η_P` is an
256	isomorphism of sheaves of modules (the reflective-localization unit becomes invertible
257	after sheafifying).  Obtained by feeding `localIso_toPresheaf_map_unit` through the
258	localization criterion `isIso_sheafification_map_iff`.  Project-local: the `m = 0`
259	launching pad and the un-whiskered special case of
260	`isIso_sheafification_whiskerRight_unit`. -/
261	lemma isIso_sheafification_map_unit (P : X.PresheafOfModules) :
262	    IsIso (sheafification.map
263	      ((PresheafOfModules.sheafificationAdjunction (𝟙 X.ringCatSheaf.obj)).unit.app P)) :=
264	  (isIso_sheafification_map_iff _).mpr (localIso_toPresheaf_map_unit P)
265	
266	/-! ## Project-local Mathlib supplement — relative tensor product as a coequalizer
267	
268	This section builds the **objectwise** content of `lem:relativeTensor_as_coequalizer`
269	(`relativeTensorCoequalizerIso`): over a commutative ring `S` and `S`-modules `M, N`,
270	the relative tensor product `M ⊗[S] N` is the coequalizer, *in the category of abelian
271	groups*, of the two `S`-action maps
272	
273	  `M ⊗[ℤ] S ⊗[ℤ] N  ⇉  M ⊗[ℤ] N`,    `m ⊗ s ⊗ n ↦ (s • m) ⊗ n`  /  `m ⊗ (s • n)`.
274	
275	This is the Mathlib-absent brick on which the strong-monoidality comparison
276	`isIso_sheafification_whiskerRight_unit` rests: the underlying abelian presheaf of the
277	presheaf-level relative tensor `P ⊗_p Q` is, objectwise, exactly this coequalizer.  The
278	universal property is the abelian-group universal property of the relative tensor product,
279	packaged by `TensorProduct.liftAddHom`.  Everything here is axiom-clean.
280	
281	The promotion of this objectwise colimit to the presheaf category `Cᵒᵖ ⥤ AddCommGrp`
282	(where colimits are computed objectwise) and the identification of the whiskered unit
283	`η_P ▷ Q` with the induced map of coequalizers are the next steps; see the handoff note. -/
284	
285	namespace RelativeTensorCoequalizer
286	
287	open TensorProduct
288	
289	variable (S : Type u) [CommRing S] (M N : Type u)
290	  [AddCommGroup M] [Module S M] [AddCommGroup N] [Module S N]
291	
292	/-- The `S`-action map `S ⊗[ℤ] N → N`, `s ⊗ n ↦ s • n`, as a `ℤ`-linear map. -/
293	noncomputable def actN : (S ⊗[ℤ] N) →ₗ[ℤ] N :=
294	  TensorProduct.lift (LinearMap.mk₂ ℤ (fun s n => s • n)
295	    (fun s1 s2 n => add_smul s1 s2 n) (fun c s n => smul_assoc c s n)
296	    (fun s n1 n2 => smul_add s n1 n2) (fun c s n => smul_comm s c n))
297	
298	/-- The `S`-action map `M ⊗[ℤ] S → M`, `m ⊗ s ↦ s • m`, as a `ℤ`-linear map. -/
299	noncomputable def actM : (M ⊗[ℤ] S) →ₗ[ℤ] M :=
300	  TensorProduct.lift (LinearMap.mk₂ ℤ (fun m s => s • m)
301	    (fun m1 m2 s => smul_add s m1 m2) (fun c m s => smul_comm s c m)
302	    (fun m s1 s2 => add_smul s1 s2 m) (fun c m s => smul_assoc c s m))
303	
304	/-- Right action map `M ⊗[ℤ] (S ⊗[ℤ] N) → M ⊗[ℤ] N`, `m ⊗ (s ⊗ n) ↦ m ⊗ (s • n)`. -/
305	noncomputable def actRmap : (M ⊗[ℤ] (S ⊗[ℤ] N)) →ₗ[ℤ] (M ⊗[ℤ] N) :=
306	  TensorProduct.map LinearMap.id (actN S N)
307	
308	/-- Left action map `M ⊗[ℤ] (S ⊗[ℤ] N) → M ⊗[ℤ] N`, `m ⊗ (s ⊗ n) ↦ (s • m) ⊗ n`. -/
309	noncomputable def actLmap : (M ⊗[ℤ] (S ⊗[ℤ] N)) →ₗ[ℤ] (M ⊗[ℤ] N) :=
310	  (TensorProduct.map (actM S M) LinearMap.id).comp
311	    (TensorProduct.assoc ℤ M S N).symm.toLinearMap
312	
313	omit [Module S M] in
314	@[simp] lemma actRmap_tmul (m : M) (s : S) (n : N) :
315	    actRmap S M N (m ⊗ₜ (s ⊗ₜ n)) = m ⊗ₜ (s • n) := rfl
316	
317	omit [Module S N] in
318	@[simp] lemma actLmap_tmul (m : M) (s : S) (n : N) :
319	    actLmap S M N (m ⊗ₜ (s ⊗ₜ n)) = (s • m) ⊗ₜ n := rfl
320	
321	/-- The canonical projection `M ⊗[ℤ] N → M ⊗[S] N`, `m ⊗ n ↦ m ⊗ n`, as a `ℤ`-linear
322	map.  It is the cofork map exhibiting `M ⊗[S] N` as the coequalizer. -/
323	noncomputable def projL : (M ⊗[ℤ] N) →ₗ[ℤ] (M ⊗[S] N) :=
324	  (TensorProduct.liftAddHom
325	    { toFun := fun m =>
326	        (LinearMap.toAddMonoidHom (((TensorProduct.mk S M N) m).restrictScalars ℤ))
327	      map_zero' := by ext n; simp
328	      map_add' := fun m1 m2 => by ext n; simp }
329	    (fun r m n => by simp)).toIntLinearMap
330	
331	@[simp] lemma projL_tmul (m : M) (n : N) : projL S M N (m ⊗ₜ n) = m ⊗ₜ[S] n := rfl
332	
333	/-- The projection `M ⊗[ℤ] N → M ⊗[S] N` is surjective (it is the canonical
334	quotient map onto the relative tensor). -/
335	lemma projL_surjective : Function.Surjective (projL S M N) := by
336	  intro y
337	  induction y using TensorProduct.induction_on with
338	  | zero => exact ⟨0, map_zero _⟩
339	  | tmul m n => exact ⟨m ⊗ₜ[ℤ] n, projL_tmul S M N m n⟩
340	  | add a b ha hb =>
341	    obtain ⟨pa, rfl⟩ := ha; obtain ⟨pb, rfl⟩ := hb; exact ⟨pa + pb, map_add _ _ _⟩
342	
343	/-- The two action maps become equal after the projection: this is the cofork
344	coequalizing condition, established at the level of `ℤ`-linear maps. -/
345	lemma projL_comp_act :
346	    (projL S M N).comp (actLmap S M N) = (projL S M N).comp (actRmap S M N) := by
347	  apply TensorProduct.ext'; intro m x
348	  induction x with
349	  | zero => rw [tmul_zero, map_zero, map_zero]
350	  | tmul s n =>
351	    change projL S M N (actLmap S M N (m ⊗ₜ (s ⊗ₜ n)))
352	      = projL S M N (actRmap S M N (m ⊗ₜ (s ⊗ₜ n)))
353	    rw [actLmap_tmul, actRmap_tmul, projL_tmul, projL_tmul, ← TensorProduct.smul_tmul',
354	      TensorProduct.tmul_smul]
355	  | add a b ha hb => rw [tmul_add, map_add, map_add, ha, hb]
356	
357	/-- Left action map as a morphism of abelian groups. -/
358	noncomputable def aL :
359	    AddCommGrpCat.of (M ⊗[ℤ] (S ⊗[ℤ] N)) ⟶ AddCommGrpCat.of (M ⊗[ℤ] N) :=
360	  AddCommGrpCat.ofHom (actLmap S M N).toAddMonoidHom
361	/-- Right action map as a morphism of abelian groups. -/
362	noncomputable def aR :
363	    AddCommGrpCat.of (M ⊗[ℤ] (S ⊗[ℤ] N)) ⟶ AddCommGrpCat.of (M ⊗[ℤ] N) :=
364	  AddCommGrpCat.ofHom (actRmap S M N).toAddMonoidHom
365	/-- The projection as a morphism of abelian groups. -/
366	noncomputable def piMor :
367	    AddCommGrpCat.of (M ⊗[ℤ] N) ⟶ AddCommGrpCat.of (M ⊗[S] N) :=
368	  AddCommGrpCat.ofHom (projL S M N).toAddMonoidHom
369	
370	@[simp] lemma piMor_apply (x) : (ConcreteCategory.hom (piMor S M N)) x = projL S M N x := rfl
371	
372	instance piMor_epi : Epi (piMor S M N) :=
373	  ConcreteCategory.epi_of_surjective (piMor S M N) (projL_surjective S M N)
374	
375	/-- The projection coequalizes the two action maps (as morphisms of abelian groups). -/
376	lemma coeq_condition : aL S M N ≫ piMor S M N = aR S M N ≫ piMor S M N := by
377	  ext x; exact LinearMap.congr_fun (projL_comp_act S M N) x
378	
379	/-- The cofork `M ⊗[ℤ] (S ⊗[ℤ] N) ⇉ M ⊗[ℤ] N → M ⊗[S] N` of abelian groups. -/
380	noncomputable def cofork : Limits.Cofork (aL S M N) (aR S M N) :=
381	  Limits.Cofork.ofπ (piMor S M N) (coeq_condition S M N)
382	
383	/-- The descent map out of `M ⊗[S] N` induced by a cofork `s`: a pair of global
384	sections balanced under the `S`-action factors through the relative tensor.  This
385	is the universal property packaged by `TensorProduct.liftAddHom`. -/
386	noncomputable def descHom (s : Limits.Cofork (aL S M N) (aR S M N)) :
387	    (M ⊗[S] N) →+ s.pt :=
388	  TensorProduct.liftAddHom
389	    { toFun := fun m =>
390	        { toFun := fun n => (ConcreteCategory.hom s.π) (m ⊗ₜ[ℤ] n)
391	          map_zero' := by rw [tmul_zero, map_zero]
392	          map_add' := fun n1 n2 => by rw [tmul_add, map_add] }
393	      map_zero' := by ext n; simp [zero_tmul]
394	      map_add' := fun m1 m2 => by ext n; simp [add_tmul] }
395	    (fun a m n => by
396	      simp only [AddMonoidHom.coe_mk, ZeroHom.coe_mk]
397	      have key :=
398	        congrArg (fun φ => (ConcreteCategory.hom φ) (m ⊗ₜ[ℤ] (a ⊗ₜ[ℤ] n))) s.condition
399	      simpa using key)
400	
401	@[simp] lemma descHom_tmul (s : Limits.Cofork (aL S M N) (aR S M N)) (m : M) (n : N) :
402	    descHom S M N s (m ⊗ₜ[S] n) = (ConcreteCategory.hom s.π) (m ⊗ₜ[ℤ] n) := rfl
403	
404	/-- The descent map as a morphism of abelian groups out of the cofork apex. -/
405	noncomputable def descMor (s : Limits.Cofork (aL S M N) (aR S M N)) :
406	    (cofork S M N).pt ⟶ s.pt :=
407	  AddCommGrpCat.ofHom (descHom S M N s)
408	
409	/-- The descent map factors the cofork's projection: `π ≫ descMor s = s.π`. -/
410	lemma descFac (s : Limits.Cofork (aL S M N) (aR S M N)) :
411	    (cofork S M N).π ≫ descMor S M N s = s.π := by
412	  ext x
413	  induction x using TensorProduct.induction_on with
414	  | zero => simp
415	  | tmul m n =>
416	    change descHom S M N s (projL S M N (m ⊗ₜ[ℤ] n)) = (ConcreteCategory.hom s.π) (m ⊗ₜ[ℤ] n)
417	    rw [projL_tmul, descHom_tmul]
418	  | add a b ha hb => simp only [map_add, ha, hb]
419	
420	/-- **`M ⊗[S] N` is the coequalizer**, in the category of abelian groups, of the two
421	`S`-action maps `M ⊗[ℤ] (S ⊗[ℤ] N) ⇉ M ⊗[ℤ] N`.  This is the objectwise content of
422	`lem:relativeTensor_as_coequalizer`; uniqueness uses that the projection `piMor` is an
423	epimorphism.  Axiom-clean. -/
424	noncomputable def isColimitCofork : Limits.IsColimit (cofork S M N) :=
425	  Limits.Cofork.IsColimit.mk _ (descMor S M N) (descFac S M N)
426	    (fun s _ hf => (cancel_epi (piMor S M N)).mp (hf.trans (descFac S M N s).symm))
427	
428	end RelativeTensorCoequalizer
429	
430	/-! ## Project-local Mathlib supplement — presheaf promotion of the coequalizer (Step 1)
431	
432	The objectwise coequalizer `RelativeTensorCoequalizer.isColimitCofork` exhibits, for a fixed
433	open `U`, the relative tensor `Γ(U,P) ⊗_{R(U)} Γ(U,Q)` as a coequalizer of the two
434	`R(U)`-action maps on `Γ(U,P) ⊗_ℤ R(U) ⊗_ℤ Γ(U,Q) ⇉ Γ(U,P) ⊗_ℤ Γ(U,Q)`.  To promote this
435	to the functor category `(Opens X)ᵒᵖ ⥤ Ab` (where colimits are computed objectwise, via
436	`CategoryTheory.Limits.evaluationJointlyReflectsColimits`) one first needs the two **domain
437	presheaves of the cofork as honest functors**, whose restriction maps are the `ℤ`-tensors of
438	the underlying restriction maps.  This section builds the first of those two functors
439	(`relTensorDomainPresheaf`, the `Γ(-,P) ⊗_ℤ Γ(-,Q)` presheaf); it is the concrete Step-1 brick
440	of `lem:relativeTensor_as_coequalizer` (`relativeTensorCoequalizerIso`).
441	
442	See the handoff note at the end of the file for the verified recipe for the remaining pieces
443	(triple-tensor presheaf, the natural action/projection transformations, the colimit lift, and
444	the apex identification) and the heartbeat/coercion friction points that must be budgeted. -/
445	
446	open scoped TensorProduct
447	
448	/-- Restriction map for a presheaf of modules with syntactic `↥(P.obj U)` carriers.
449	The underlying function is `(P.presheaf.map f).hom`; the type annotation forces the
450	domain/codomain to print as `↥(P.obj U)` / `↥(P.obj V)` (not `↥((P.presheaf).obj U)`,
451	which are rfl-defeq but syntactically distinct).  The syntactic agreement is the
452	load-bearing ingredient for `TensorProduct.map_tmul` unification in
453	`relTensorActL.naturality` / `relTensorActR.naturality`. -/
454	private noncomputable def objRestrict (P : X.PresheafOfModules)
455	    {U V : (TopologicalSpace.Opens X)ᵒᵖ} (f : U ⟶ V) :
456	    ↥(P.obj U) →ₗ[ℤ] ↥(P.obj V) :=
457	  (show ↥(P.obj U) →+ ↥(P.obj V) from
458	    { toFun := (P.presheaf.map f).hom
459	      map_zero' := map_zero (P.presheaf.map f).hom
460	      map_add' := map_add (P.presheaf.map f).hom }).toIntLinearMap
461	
462	@[simp] private lemma objRestrict_apply (P : X.PresheafOfModules)
463	    {U V : (TopologicalSpace.Opens X)ᵒᵖ} (f : U ⟶ V) (x : ↥(P.obj U)) :
464	    objRestrict P f x = (P.presheaf.map f).hom x := rfl
465	
466	/-- Identity law for the syntactic-carrier restriction: `objRestrict P (𝟙 U) = id`. -/
467	private lemma objRestrict_id (P : X.PresheafOfModules) (U : (TopologicalSpace.Opens X)ᵒᵖ) :
468	    objRestrict P (𝟙 U) = LinearMap.id := by
469	  ext x
470	  simp only [objRestrict_apply, CategoryTheory.Functor.map_id, AddCommGrpCat.hom_id,
471	    AddMonoidHom.id_apply, LinearMap.id_coe, id_eq]
472	
473	/-- Composition law for the syntactic-carrier restriction:
474	`objRestrict P (f ≫ g) = (objRestrict P g) ∘ (objRestrict P f)`. -/
475	private lemma objRestrict_comp (P : X.PresheafOfModules)
476	    {U V W : (TopologicalSpace.Opens X)ᵒᵖ} (f : U ⟶ V) (g : V ⟶ W) :
477	    objRestrict P (f ≫ g) = (objRestrict P g).comp (objRestrict P f) := by
478	  ext x
479	  simp only [objRestrict_apply, CategoryTheory.Functor.map_comp, AddCommGrpCat.hom_comp,
480	    AddMonoidHom.coe_comp, Function.comp_apply, LinearMap.comp_apply]
481	
482	/-- The objectwise `ℤ`-tensor presheaf `U ↦ Γ(U,P) ⊗_ℤ Γ(U,Q)` of two presheaves of modules
483	over a scheme, as a functor into abelian groups, with restriction maps the `ℤ`-tensors of the
484	two underlying restriction maps.  This is the codomain (apex-adjacent) presheaf of the cofork
485	in the presheaf promotion of `RelativeTensorCoequalizer.isColimitCofork`; it is the concrete
486	Step-1 brick of the presheaf-level coequalizer iso `relativeTensorCoequalizerIso`
487	(`lem:relativeTensor_as_coequalizer`).  Project-local: no objectwise `ℤ`-tensor of
488	abelian-group presheaves is provided by Mathlib (`AddCommGrpCat` carries no monoidal
489	structure in the current pin). -/
490	noncomputable def relTensorDomainPresheaf (P Q : X.PresheafOfModules) :
491	    (TopologicalSpace.Opens X)ᵒᵖ ⥤ Ab where
492	  obj U := AddCommGrpCat.of (P.obj U ⊗[ℤ] Q.obj U)
493	  map {U V} f := AddCommGrpCat.ofHom
494	    (TensorProduct.map (objRestrict P f) (objRestrict Q f)).toAddMonoidHom
495	  map_id U := by
496	    ext x
497	    induction x using TensorProduct.induction_on with
498	    | zero => simp
499	    | tmul m n =>
500	      simp only [AddCommGrpCat.hom_ofHom, LinearMap.toAddMonoidHom_coe, TensorProduct.map_tmul,
501	        objRestrict_apply, CategoryTheory.Functor.map_id, AddCommGrpCat.hom_id,
502	        AddMonoidHom.id_apply]
503	    | add a b ha hb => simp only [map_add, ha, hb]
504	  map_comp {U V W} f g := by
505	    ext x
506	    induction x using TensorProduct.induction_on with
507	    | zero => simp
508	    | tmul m n =>
509	      simp only [AddCommGrpCat.hom_ofHom, LinearMap.toAddMonoidHom_coe, TensorProduct.map_tmul,
510	        objRestrict_apply, CategoryTheory.Functor.map_comp,
511	        AddCommGrpCat.hom_comp, AddMonoidHom.coe_comp, Function.comp_apply]
512	    | add a b ha hb => simp only [map_add, ha, hb]
513	
514	/-- The objectwise `ℤ`-tensor triple presheaf `U ↦ Γ(U,P) ⊗_ℤ (𝒪_X(U) ⊗_ℤ Γ(U,Q))` of two
515	presheaves of modules over a scheme, as a functor into abelian groups, with restriction maps the
516	`ℤ`-tensors of the underlying restriction maps (the middle factor restricting via the ring
517	restriction map of `𝒪_X`).  This is the **domain** row of the relative-tensor coequalizer
518	presentation (`lem:relativeTensor_as_coequalizer`); objectwise it is the triple tensor on which
519	the two `R(U)`-action maps `RelativeTensorCoequalizer.actLmap`/`actRmap` act.  Project-local: no
520	objectwise `ℤ`-tensor of abelian-group presheaves is provided by Mathlib. -/
521	noncomputable def relTensorTriplePresheaf (P Q : X.PresheafOfModules) :
522	    (TopologicalSpace.Opens X)ᵒᵖ ⥤ Ab where
523	  obj U := AddCommGrpCat.of (P.obj U ⊗[ℤ] (X.sheaf.obj.obj U ⊗[ℤ] Q.obj U))
524	  map {U V} f := AddCommGrpCat.ofHom
525	    (TensorProduct.map (objRestrict P f)
526	      (TensorProduct.map (X.sheaf.obj.map f).hom.toAddMonoidHom.toIntLinearMap
527	        (objRestrict Q f))).toAddMonoidHom
528	  map_id U := by
529	    have hR : (X.sheaf.obj.map (𝟙 U)).hom.toAddMonoidHom.toIntLinearMap =
530	        LinearMap.id (R := ℤ) (M := ↥(X.sheaf.obj.obj U)) := by
531	      ext s
532	      simp only [CategoryTheory.Functor.map_id, CommRingCat.hom_id, RingHom.toAddMonoidHom_eq_coe,
533	        AddMonoidHom.coe_toIntLinearMap, LinearMap.id_coe, id_eq]
534	      rfl
535	    rw [objRestrict_id P U, objRestrict_id Q U, hR, TensorProduct.map_id, TensorProduct.map_id]
536	    rfl
537	  map_comp {U V W} f g := by
538	    have hR : (X.sheaf.obj.map (f ≫ g)).hom.toAddMonoidHom.toIntLinearMap =
539	        ((X.sheaf.obj.map g).hom.toAddMonoidHom.toIntLinearMap).comp
540	          ((X.sheaf.obj.map f).hom.toAddMonoidHom.toIntLinearMap) := by
541	      ext s
542	      simp only [CategoryTheory.Functor.map_comp, CommRingCat.hom_comp,
543	        RingHom.toAddMonoidHom_eq_coe, AddMonoidHom.coe_toIntLinearMap, LinearMap.coe_comp,
544	        Function.comp_apply]
545	      rfl
546	    rw [objRestrict_comp P f g, objRestrict_comp Q f g, hR, TensorProduct.map_comp,
547	      TensorProduct.map_comp]
548	    rfl
549	
550	/-- The **left-action** natural transformation of the coequalizer rows
551	(`def:relTensorActL`): `relTensorTriplePresheaf P Q ⟶ relTensorDomainPresheaf P Q`, whose
552	component at `U` is the objectwise left-action map
553	`RelativeTensorCoequalizer.actLmap` collapsing the middle ring factor through the scalar
554	action of `𝒪_X(U)` on `Γ(U,P)`, `m ⊗ (s ⊗ n) ↦ (s • m) ⊗ n`.  Naturality in `U` is the
555	compatibility of the module action with the restriction maps, checked on elementary tensors
556	by `⊗`-induction (the single fact `PresheafOfModules.map_smul`, bridged to the abelian
557	restriction by `objRestrict_apply`). -/
558	noncomputable def relTensorActL (P Q : X.PresheafOfModules) :
559	    relTensorTriplePresheaf P Q ⟶ relTensorDomainPresheaf P Q where
560	  app U := AddCommGrpCat.ofHom
561	    (RelativeTensorCoequalizer.actLmap (X.sheaf.obj.obj U) (P.obj U) (Q.obj U)).toAddMonoidHom
562	  naturality {U V} f := by
563	    -- The underlying ℤ-linear naturality square, proven by `⊗`-induction.  The single
564	    -- mathematical fact is `PresheafOfModules.map_smul` (semilinearity of the restriction).
565	    have key :
566	        (RelativeTensorCoequalizer.actLmap (↥(X.sheaf.obj.obj V)) (↥(P.obj V)) (↥(Q.obj V))).comp
567	            (TensorProduct.map (objRestrict P f)
568	              (TensorProduct.map (X.sheaf.obj.map f).hom.toAddMonoidHom.toIntLinearMap
569	                (objRestrict Q f)))
570	          = (TensorProduct.map (objRestrict P f) (objRestrict Q f)).comp
571	              (RelativeTensorCoequalizer.actLmap (↥(X.sheaf.obj.obj U)) (↥(P.obj U))
572	                (↥(Q.obj U))) := by
573	      apply TensorProduct.ext'
574	      intro m y
575	      induction y using TensorProduct.induction_on with
576	      | zero => simp
577	      | tmul s n =>
578	        change ((X.sheaf.obj.map f).hom.toAddMonoidHom.toIntLinearMap s • objRestrict P f m)
579	              ⊗ₜ[ℤ] objRestrict Q f n
580	            = objRestrict P f (s • m) ⊗ₜ[ℤ] objRestrict Q f n
581	        congr 1
582	        rw [objRestrict_apply, objRestrict_apply]
583	        exact (PresheafOfModules.map_smul P f s m).symm
584	      | add a b ha hb => simp only [map_add, ha, hb, TensorProduct.tmul_add]
585	    -- Transport the linear-map square to the categorical naturality square in `Ab`.
586	    apply AddCommGrpCat.hom_ext
587	    dsimp only [relTensorTriplePresheaf, relTensorDomainPresheaf]
588	    ext z
589	    have hz := LinearMap.congr_fun key z
590	    simpa only [AddCommGrpCat.hom_comp, AddCommGrpCat.hom_ofHom, AddMonoidHom.comp_apply,
591	      LinearMap.toAddMonoidHom_coe, LinearMap.comp_apply] using hz
592	
593	/-- The **right-action** natural transformation of the coequalizer rows
594	(`def:relTensorActR`): `relTensorTriplePresheaf P Q ⟶ relTensorDomainPresheaf P Q`, whose
595	component at `U` is the objectwise right-action map
596	`RelativeTensorCoequalizer.actRmap` collapsing the middle ring factor through the scalar
597	action of `𝒪_X(U)` on `Γ(U,Q)`, `m ⊗ (s ⊗ n) ↦ m ⊗ (s • n)`.  Naturality is the
598	compatibility of the module action with the restriction maps (`PresheafOfModules.map_smul`
599	on `Q`), checked on elementary tensors by `⊗`-induction. -/
600	noncomputable def relTensorActR (P Q : X.PresheafOfModules) :
601	    relTensorTriplePresheaf P Q ⟶ relTensorDomainPresheaf P Q where
602	  app U := AddCommGrpCat.ofHom
603	    (RelativeTensorCoequalizer.actRmap (X.sheaf.obj.obj U) (P.obj U) (Q.obj U)).toAddMonoidHom
604	  naturality {U V} f := by
605	    have key :
606	        (RelativeTensorCoequalizer.actRmap (↥(X.sheaf.obj.obj V)) (↥(P.obj V)) (↥(Q.obj V))).comp
607	            (TensorProduct.map (objRestrict P f)
608	              (TensorProduct.map (X.sheaf.obj.map f).hom.toAddMonoidHom.toIntLinearMap
609	                (objRestrict Q f)))
610	          = (TensorProduct.map (objRestrict P f) (objRestrict Q f)).comp
611	              (RelativeTensorCoequalizer.actRmap (↥(X.sheaf.obj.obj U)) (↥(P.obj U))
612	                (↥(Q.obj U))) := by
613	      apply TensorProduct.ext'
614	      intro m y
615	      induction y using TensorProduct.induction_on with
616	      | zero => simp
617	      | tmul s n =>
618	        change objRestrict P f m
619	              ⊗ₜ[ℤ] ((X.sheaf.obj.map f).hom.toAddMonoidHom.toIntLinearMap s • objRestrict Q f n)
620	            = objRestrict P f m ⊗ₜ[ℤ] objRestrict Q f (s • n)
621	        congr 1
622	        rw [objRestrict_apply, objRestrict_apply]
623	        exact (PresheafOfModules.map_smul Q f s n).symm
624	      | add a b ha hb => simp only [map_add, ha, hb, TensorProduct.tmul_add]
625	    apply AddCommGrpCat.hom_ext
626	    dsimp only [relTensorTriplePresheaf, relTensorDomainPresheaf]
627	    ext z
628	    have hz := LinearMap.congr_fun key z
629	    simpa only [AddCommGrpCat.hom_comp, AddCommGrpCat.hom_ofHom, AddMonoidHom.comp_apply,
630	      LinearMap.toAddMonoidHom_coe, LinearMap.comp_apply] using hz
631	
632	/-- The **projection** natural transformation (`relTensorProj`):
633	`relTensorDomainPresheaf P Q ⟶ (toPresheaf).obj (P ⊗_p Q)`, whose component at `U` is the
634	canonical quotient `RelativeTensorCoequalizer.projL` from the objectwise `ℤ`-tensor onto the
635	relative tensor `Γ(U,P) ⊗_{𝒪_X(U)} Γ(U,Q)` (the apex of the cofork, identified with the value of
636	the presheaf monoidal tensor by `PresheafOfModules.Monoidal.tensorObj_obj`).  This is the cofork
637	map of the presheaf-level coequalizer presentation `relativeTensorCoequalizerIso`. -/
638	noncomputable def relTensorProj (P Q : X.PresheafOfModules) :
639	    relTensorDomainPresheaf P Q ⟶
640	      (PresheafOfModules.toPresheaf X.ringCatSheaf.obj).obj
641	        (MonoidalCategory.tensorObj (C := MonoidalPresheaf X) P Q) where
642	  app U := AddCommGrpCat.ofHom
643	    (RelativeTensorCoequalizer.projL (X.sheaf.obj.obj U) (P.obj U) (Q.obj U)).toAddMonoidHom
644	  naturality {U V} f := by
645	    -- NATURALITY (the square `projL_V ∘ domain.map f = apex.map f ∘ projL_U`).  We prove the
646	    -- underlying `ℤ`-linear square as `key` and transport it to the categorical square in `Ab`.
647	    -- An element-level `⊗`-induction at the `Ab` level is blocked by the `AddCommGrpCat.of` carrier
648	    -- instance mismatch (`map_add` fails to fire on the bundled `Ab`-morphism applied to `a + b`);
649	    -- working with bare `ℤ`-linear maps and `TensorProduct.ext'` sidesteps it entirely.  On an
650	    -- elementary tensor `m ⊗ₜ n` both composites send it to
651	    -- `(objRestrict P f m) ⊗ₜ[R(V)] (objRestrict Q f n)` definitionally: the LHS via
652	    -- `TensorProduct.map`+`projL`, the RHS via `projL`+`tensorObj_map_tmul`
653	    -- (both `⊗ₜ`-on-the-nose).  The `S = X.sheaf.obj.obj V` vs `R.obj V` base-ring discrepancy is a
654	    -- `forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat`-identity, so the elementary-tensor case is `rfl` (no instance
655	    -- re-synthesis, since the existing goal instances are reused).
656	    have key :
657	        (RelativeTensorCoequalizer.projL (↑(X.sheaf.obj.obj V)) (↑(P.obj V)) (↑(Q.obj V))).comp
658	            (TensorProduct.map (objRestrict P f) (objRestrict Q f))
659	          = (AddCommGrpCat.Hom.hom
660	                (((PresheafOfModules.toPresheaf X.ringCatSheaf.obj).obj
661	                  (MonoidalCategory.tensorObj (C := MonoidalPresheaf X) P Q)).map
662	                    f)).toIntLinearMap.comp
663	              (RelativeTensorCoequalizer.projL (↑(X.sheaf.obj.obj U)) (↑(P.obj U))
664	                (↑(Q.obj U))) := by
665	      apply TensorProduct.ext'
666	      intro m n
667	      rfl
668	    apply AddCommGrpCat.hom_ext
669	    ext z
670	    have hz := LinearMap.congr_fun key z
671	    simpa only [AddCommGrpCat.hom_comp, AddCommGrpCat.hom_ofHom, AddMonoidHom.comp_apply,
672	      LinearMap.toAddMonoidHom_coe, LinearMap.comp_apply, AddMonoidHom.coe_toIntLinearMap] using hz
673	
674	/-- The cofork condition for the presheaf-level relative-tensor coequalizer: the left- and
675	right-action rows compose equally with the projection, `a_L ≫ π = a_R ≫ π`, as natural
676	transformations of `(Opens X)ᵒᵖ ⥤ Ab`.  Objectwise it is
677	`RelativeTensorCoequalizer.coeq_condition`. -/
678	lemma relTensorActL_proj_eq (P Q : X.PresheafOfModules) :
679	    relTensorActL P Q ≫ relTensorProj P Q = relTensorActR P Q ≫ relTensorProj P Q := by
680	  ext U : 2
681	  exact RelativeTensorCoequalizer.coeq_condition (X.sheaf.obj.obj U) (P.obj U) (Q.obj U)
682	
683	/- Planner strategy: 3-step promotion (blueprint `lem:relativeTensor_as_coequalizer` proof):
684	1. OBJECTWISE — at each `U`, instantiate `RelativeTensorCoequalizer.isColimitCofork` with
685	   `S = O_X(U)`, `M = P(U)`, `N = Q(U)`. (API DONE axiom-clean.)
686	2. PROMOTE — the three objectwise families ARE `relTensorActL`/`relTensorActR`/`relTensorProj`
687	   (already natural). A functor-category cocone is a colimit iff every evaluation is, via
688	   `CategoryTheory.Limits.evaluationJointlyReflectsColimits` [Mathlib, verify with leansearch].
689	   NOTE (iter-063): leansearch only finds `CategoryTheory.Limits.evaluationJointlyReflectsLimits`
690	   (limits), not the colimit version; the colimit analogue may be
691	   `PresheafOfModules.evaluationJointlyReflectsColimits` or
692	   `CategoryTheory.Limits.combinedIsColimit` — verify before use.
693	3. APEX — identify the apex presheaf `U ↦ P(U) ⊗_{O_X(U)} Q(U)` with the underlying Ab-presheaf
694	   of `P ⊗_p Q` via `PresheafOfModules.Monoidal.tensorObj_obj` (verified in Mathlib);
695	   transport the colimit along it.

866	  (left unitor `unitModule X ⊗ L^{⊗m'} ≅ L^{⊗m'}`, transported along `0 + m' = m'`).
867	
868	* **inductive step `m = k+1`** — needs the sheaf-level **associator**
869	  `tensorObj (tensorObj A B) C ≅ tensorObj A (tensorObj B C)`.  Concretely, with
870	  `A = L^{⊗k}`, `B = L^{⊗m'}`, one must produce
871	  `(A ⊗ L) ⊗ B ≅ (A ⊗ B) ⊗ L` (= associator, then `tensorBraiding`, then
872	  associator⁻¹), combine with the inductive hypothesis `L^{⊗(k+m')} ≅ A ⊗ B`

973	arbitrary presheaf in the **pointwise** monoidal structure on `Cᵒᵖ ⥤ A` whenever `A` is

976	from `ztensor_whisker_localIso`:
977	
978	* `Ab` carries no (tensor) monoidal structure in Mathlib, and the `ModuleCat` monoidal
979	  structure insists that ring and modules live in the same universe.  We therefore work in
980	  `ModuleCat.{u} (ULift.{u} ℤ)` and transport `J.W` along the carrier-preserving
981	  equivalence `modToAb` (an equivalence is a left adjoint in both directions, hence
982	  preserves sheafification both ways — `W_whiskerRight_modToAb_iff`).
983	* the morphism in `ztensor_whisker_localIso` is the *relative*-tensor whiskering
984	  `f ▷ R` (over `𝒪_X`), not the `ℤ`-tensor one.  The coequalizer presentation
985	  `relativeTensorCoequalizerIso` exhibits its underlying abelian map as the map induced on
986	  coequalizers by the two `ℤ`-tensor whiskered rows (`domWhisker`, `tripWhisker`); abelian
987	  sheafification preserves the coequalizers and inverts the rows, hence inverts the induced
988	  map (`GrothendieckTopology.W_iff`).
989	-/

1042	/-- The presheaf of `ULift ℤ`-modules underlying a presheaf of `𝒪_X`-modules, with the
1043	syntactic `↥(P.obj U)` carriers of `objRestrict`.  This places the underlying abelian
1044	presheaf of `P` in a category (`Cᵒᵖ ⥤ ModuleCat (ULift ℤ)`) which Mathlib equips with a
1045	pointwise braided monoidal-closed structure, so that
1046	`GrothendieckTopology.W.whiskerRight` applies. -/
1047	private noncomputable def uModPresheaf (P : X.PresheafOfModules) :
1048	    (TopologicalSpace.Opens X)ᵒᵖ ⥤ ModuleCat.{u} (ULift.{u} ℤ) where
1049	  obj U := ModuleCat.of (ULift.{u} ℤ) ↥(P.obj U)
1050	  map {U V} g := ModuleCat.ofHom (toULiftIntLinearMap (objRestrict P g).toAddMonoidHom)
1051	  map_id U := by
1052	    ext x
1053	    exact LinearMap.congr_fun (objRestrict_id P U) x
1054	  map_comp {U V W} g h := by
1055	    ext x
1056	    exact LinearMap.congr_fun (objRestrict_comp P g h) x

1062	  map {U V} g := ModuleCat.ofHom

1064	  map_id U := by

1066	    change (X.sheaf.obj.map (𝟙 U)).hom s = s
1067	    rw [CategoryTheory.Functor.map_id]
1068	    rfl

1070	    ext s
1071	    change (X.sheaf.obj.map (g ≫ h)).hom s

1103	    (J : GrothendieckTopology C) {F G : Cᵒᵖ ⥤ ModuleCat.{u} (ULift.{u} ℤ)} (ψ : F ⟶ G) :
1104	    J.W (Functor.whiskerRight ψ modToAb.{u}) ↔ J.W ψ := by

1106	    Sheaf.preservesSheafification_of_adjunction J modToAb.{u}.asEquivalence.toAdjunction

1109	  constructor

1112	    refine ((J.W).arrow_mk_iso_iff ?_).mp h2
1113	    refine Arrow.isoMk
1114	      (NatIso.ofComponents
1115	        (fun U => modToAb.{u}.asEquivalence.unitIso.symm.app (F.obj U)) ?_)
1116	      (NatIso.ofComponents
1117	        (fun U => modToAb.{u}.asEquivalence.unitIso.symm.app (G.obj U)) ?_) ?_

1119	      exact modToAb.{u}.asEquivalence.unitIso.inv.naturality (F.map g)

1122	    · ext U : 2

1124	      exact (modToAb.{u}.asEquivalence.unitIso.inv.naturality (ψ.app U)).symm
1125	  · intro h
1126	    exact J.W_of_preservesSheafification modToAb.{u} _ h

128	/-! ## §2. The Quot functor
129	
130	The Quot functor `Quot^{Φ,L}_{E/X/S}` sends an `S`-scheme `T ⟶ S` to the
131	set of equivalence classes `⟨F, q⟩` of pairs `(F, q)` where
132	- `F` is a coherent sheaf on `X_T = X ×_S T` whose schematic support is
133	  proper over `T` and which is `T`-flat,
134	- `q : E_T ↠ F` is a surjective `O_{X_T}`-linear homomorphism,
135	- the fiberwise Hilbert polynomial of `F|_{X_t}` with respect to `L|_{X_t}`
136	  equals `Φ` at every `t ∈ T`.
137	
138	Two pairs `(F, q)` and `(F', q')` are equivalent iff `ker(q) = ker(q')`.
139	
140	The Hilbert scheme is the special case `E = O_X`:
141	`Hilb^{Φ,L}_{X/S} = Quot^{Φ,L}_{O_X/X/S}`.
142	
143	Blueprint reference: `def:quot_functor` (Nitsure §1; FGA Explained Ch. 5). -/
